basic_details.job_title: SBI PO 2026 Notification PDF Out - Apply Online for 1,500 Probationary Officer Posts
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Prelims → Mains → Psychometric Test, Group Exercise & Interview
basic_details.short_information: State Bank of India has released the official notification for SBI PO Recruitment 2026, inviting online applications for 1500 Probationary Officer (PO) vacancies under Advertisement No. CRPD/PO/2026-27/09. Online registration is open from 18 June 2026 to 8 July 2026. Graduates in any discipline aged 21 to 30 years (as on 01 April 2026) can apply through SBI's official careers portal, with selection through Prelims, Mains, and an Interview process.

application_fee.details: The application fee for SBI PO Recruitment 2026 is Rs 750 for Unreserved (UR), EWS, and OBC candidates, while SC, ST, and PwBD candidates are exempted from paying any fee. This fee is non-refundable once paid and cannot be carried forward or held in reserve for any other examination, so candidates should confirm their category and details before making the payment. | SBI PO Previous Year Question Papers: Download PDF Here

age_limit.details: Candidates applying for SBI PO Recruitment 2026 must be not below 21 years and not above 30 years as on 01 April 2026, meaning they must have been born between 02 April 1996 and 01 April 2005 (both dates inclusive). Age relaxation is available to specific categories, but the notification clarifies that cumulative age relaxation across multiple categories is not permitted. | SBI PO Age Limit 2026: Category Wise Relaxation & Eligibility

qualification.details: Candidates need a Graduation degree in any discipline from a recognised university, or an equivalent qualification recognised by the Central Government, as on 30 September 2026. Final-year or final-semester students can also apply provisionally, but they must produce proof of having passed the graduation examination on or before 30 September 2026 if shortlisted for interview. Candidates holding professional qualifications such as Medical, Engineering, Chartered Accountancy, or Cost Accountancy, as well as those with an Integrated Dual Degree (IDD) passed by the cut-off date, are also eligible.

vacancy_details.PwBD Category: d & e (ASD-Mild / Mental Illness / Multiple Disabilities)

pwd_wise_breakdown.pwbd_category: d & e (ASD-Mild / Mental Illness / Multiple Disabilities)

salary_details.details: Selected candidates for SBI PO Recruitment 2026 will draw a starting basic pay of Rs 48,480, plus 4 advance increments, in the Junior Management Grade Scale-I (JMGS-I) pay scale of 48480-2000/7-62480-2340/2-67160-2680/7-85920. In addition to basic pay, officers are eligible for Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), City Compensatory Allowance (CCA), Provident Fund, the National Pension System (NPS), Leave Fare Concession (LFC), medical facilities, lease rental facility, and other allowances as per the Bank's prevailing rules. The approximate CTC at the initial pay scale for the Mumbai Centre works out to Rs 21.97 Lacs. | In-Hand Salary Calculator 2026

Selection for SBI PO Recruitment 2026 follows a three-phase process: Phase-I Preliminary Examination
Phase-II Main Examination
and Phase-III Psychometric Test
Group Exercise & Interview. Candidates must clear each phase to be considered for the next
and only the marks from Phase-II and Phase-III are carried forward to prepare the final merit list.
The Preliminary Examination is an online objective test carrying 100 marks
to be completed in 1 hour
with separate timing for each of its three sections. There is no sectional cut-off in the Preliminary Exam
but a category-wise merit list is prepared based on the aggregate score
and candidates numbering approximately 10 times the vacancies in each category are shortlisted for the Main Exam.
The Main Examination is conducted online and consists of an Objective Test of 200 marks and a Descriptive Test of 30 marks
taken together in 3 hours 30 minutes. The descriptive paper is administered immediately after the objective test
and candidates must type their answers directly on the computer.
Each candidate must score a minimum qualifying mark in every individual section
including the descriptive paper
with sectional cut-offs decided by the Bank based on vacancies. A category-wise merit list is then prepared from the aggregate Main Exam score
and candidates numbering approximately 3 times the category-wise vacancies are shortlisted for Phase-III.
There is also a penalty for wrong answers in both the Preliminary and Main objective tests: for every wrong answer
1/4th of the marks assigned to that question is deducted
while unattempted questions carry no penalty.
Shortlisted candidates undergo a Psychometric Test for personality profiling
followed by a Group Exercise (20 marks) and a Personal Interview (30 marks)
totalling 50 marks. The Psychometric Test findings are shared with the interview panel to give a fuller perspective on each candidate
and all questions in this test are mandatory.
The marks from Phase-II (Main Exam

both Objective and Descriptive) and Phase-III (Group Exercise & Interview) are normalised to 100 marks each before being combined into the final merit list; marks from the Preliminary Exam are not added at this stage.
If two or more candidates score identical marks at the cut-off point in the final merit list
they are ranked according to age in descending order
meaning the older candidate is placed higher.

content_sections.heading: SBI PO Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF Out
content_sections.text: Download SBI PO Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: Why Join SBI as a Probationary Officer?
content_sections.text: This section answers why thousands of graduates target the SBI PO exam every year as one of India's most sought-after banking careers. Selected candidates step into leadership-track roles from day one, with the bank highlighting accelerated career progression, nationwide and international postings, structured leadership-development training, and exposure to modern digital banking operations. The role also comes with strong job security, an attractive compensation package, and a clear promotion path that can take meritorious officers up to Top Management Grade over time.

content_sections.heading: SBI PO Important Dates 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates need to track a single, fairly long registration window for this recruitment, along with the tentative dates for each subsequent exam phase. Online registration and fee payment run from 18 June 2026 to 8 July 2026 , and the bank has also released a tentative schedule for the Preliminary Examination, Main Examination, and the final interview stage so candidates can plan their preparation timeline in advance.

Download of Main Exam Call Letter

content_sections.heading: SBI PO Vacancy 2026: Category-Wise Breakdown of 1500 Posts
content_sections.text: SBI has notified a total of 1500 Probationary Officer vacancies, made up of 1446 regular vacancies and 54 backlog vacancies, spread across the SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories. The Unreserved (UR) category carries the largest share with 588 vacancies, followed by OBC with 390, SC with 234 (including 18 backlog), ST with 144 (including 36 backlog), and EWS with 144 vacancies. The bank has clarified that these numbers are provisional and may vary based on its actual requirement. A separate horizontal reservation is available for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) within the above categories, in line with Section 34 of the RPwD Act, 2016. A total of 15 vacancies are reserved for Visually Impaired (VI), 16 for Hearing Impaired (HI), 14 for Locomotor Disability (LD), and 16 for the combined "d&e" category covering Autism Spectrum Disorder-Mild, Mental Illness, and Multiple Disabilities.

rows.pwbd_category: d & e (ASD-Mild / Mental Illness / Multiple Disabilities)

content_sections.heading: SBI PO Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Eligibility for the SBI PO post is built on two pillars: an age limit checked as on a fixed cut-off date, and an academic qualification checked as on a separate, later cut-off date. Candidates must satisfy both conditions independently to be considered eligible for this recruitment.

content_sections.heading: SBI PO Age Limit 2026: 21 to 30 Years
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for SBI PO Recruitment 2026 must be not below 21 years and not above 30 years as on 01 April 2026 , meaning they must have been born between 02 April 1996 and 01 April 2005 (both dates inclusive). Age relaxation is available to specific categories, but the notification clarifies that cumulative age relaxation across multiple categories is not permitted. SBI PO Age Limit 2026: Category Wise Relaxation & Eligibility

content_sections.heading: SBI PO Educational Qualification 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates need a Graduation degree in any discipline from a recognised university, or an equivalent qualification recognised by the Central Government, as on 30 September 2026 . Final-year or final-semester students can also apply provisionally, but they must produce proof of having passed the graduation examination on or before 30 September 2026 if shortlisted for interview. Candidates holding professional qualifications such as Medical, Engineering, Chartered Accountancy, or Cost Accountancy, as well as those with an Integrated Dual Degree (IDD) passed by the cut-off date, are also eligible.

content_sections.heading: Number of Attempts Allowed in SBI PO Exam
content_sections.text: SBI has fixed a maximum number of attempts permissible for this exam based on category, and candidates who have already exhausted their permissible chances are not eligible to apply again. Appearing in the Main Examination counts as one attempt, while appearing only in the Preliminary Examination does not count as an attempt.

content_sections.heading: SBI PO Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: The application fee for SBI PO Recruitment 2026 is Rs 750 for Unreserved (UR), EWS, and OBC candidates , while SC, ST, and PwBD candidates are exempted from paying any fee . This fee is non-refundable once paid and cannot be carried forward or held in reserve for any other examination, so candidates should confirm their category and details before making the payment. SBI PO Previous Year Question Papers: Download PDF Here

content_sections.heading: CIBIL Score Requirement for SBI PO Candidates
content_sections.text: A distinctive eligibility condition for this recruitment relates to credit history, since SBI PO is a banking role with financial responsibility. Candidates who have defaulted on repayment under any loan, NBFC, or financial institution arrangement, including credit card dues , and whose accounts reflect adverse credit history in CIBIL or another recognised credit agency, must regularise or repay all outstanding dues on or before the date of joining the Bank . If the adverse status has not been updated in CIBIL records by the joining date, candidates must either get the status updated or produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the lender confirming there are no outstanding dues; failure to do so will render the candidate ineligible and the offer of appointment will be withdrawn. Candidates are advised to check their CIBIL score before applying.

content_sections.heading: SBI PO Selection Process 2026: Prelims, Mains & Interview
content_sections.text: Selection for SBI PO Recruitment 2026 follows a three-phase process : Phase-I Preliminary Examination, Phase-II Main Examination, and Phase-III Psychometric Test, Group Exercise & Interview. Candidates must clear each phase to be considered for the next, and only the marks from Phase-II and Phase-III are carried forward to prepare the final merit list. The Preliminary Examination is an online objective test carrying 100 marks , to be completed in 1 hour , with separate timing for each of its three sections. There is no sectional cut-off in the Preliminary Exam, but a category-wise merit list is prepared based on the aggregate score, and candidates numbering approximately 10 times the vacancies in each category are shortlisted for the Main Exam. The Main Examination is conducted online and consists of an Objective Test of 200 marks and a Descriptive Test of 30 marks , taken together in 3 hours 30 minutes . The descriptive paper is administered immediately after the objective test, and candidates must type their answers directly on the computer. Each candidate must score a minimum qualifying mark in every individual section, including the descriptive paper, with sectional cut-offs decided by the Bank based on vacancies. A category-wise merit list is then prepared from the aggregate Main Exam score, and candidates numbering approximately 3 times the category-wise vacancies are shortlisted for Phase-III. There is also a penalty for wrong answers in both the Preliminary and Main objective tests: for every wrong answer, 1/4th of the marks assigned to that question is deducted, while unattempted questions carry no penalty. Shortlisted candidates undergo a Psychometric Test for personality profiling, followed by a Group Exercise (20 marks) and a Personal Interview (30 marks) , totalling 50 marks . The Psychometric Test findings are shared with the interview panel to give a fuller perspective on each candidate, and all questions in this test are mandatory. The marks from Phase-II (Main Exam, both Objective and Descriptive) and Phase-III (Group Exercise & Interview) are normalised to 100 marks each before being combined into the final merit list; marks from the Preliminary Exam are not added at this stage. If two or more candidates score identical marks at the cut-off point in the final merit list, they are ranked according to age in descending order , meaning the older candidate is placed higher. SBI PO Syllabus 2026: Check Exam Pattern and Syllabus Details Here

content_sections.heading: SBI PO Apply Online 2026: Step-by-Step Registration Guide
content_sections.text: Candidates can apply for SBI PO Recruitment 2026 only in online mode , from 18 June 2026 to 8 July 2026 ; no other mode of application is accepted. Before starting, candidates must have a valid personal email ID and mobile number kept active until the declaration of the final result, since all call letters and result-related communication will be sent there. You can also apply directly through the dedicated SBI PO registration link at https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/sbipojun26/ .

content_sections.heading: SBI PO Salary 2026: Pay Scale and CTC
content_sections.text: Selected candidates for SBI PO Recruitment 2026 will draw a starting basic pay of Rs 48,480 , plus 4 advance increments , in the Junior Management Grade Scale-I (JMGS-I) pay scale of 48480-2000/7-62480-2340/2-67160-2680/7-85920 . In addition to basic pay, officers are eligible for Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), City Compensatory Allowance (CCA), Provident Fund, the National Pension System (NPS), Leave Fare Concession (LFC), medical facilities, lease rental facility, and other allowances as per the Bank's prevailing rules. The approximate CTC at the initial pay scale for the Mumbai Centre works out to Rs 21.97 Lacs . In-Hand Salary Calculator 2026

content_sections.heading: Joining, Bond and Probation Period
content_sections.text: Selected candidates must complete a basic banking knowledge online course before joining and will be designated as Probationary Officers , placed on probation for two years with continuous performance assessment. At the time of joining, candidates must execute a bond of Rs 2.00 Lacs , committing to serve the Bank for a minimum period of three years ; this bond is invoked if the candidate resigns before completing three years of service. Officers who clear the probation assessment standards are confirmed in JMGS-I, and SBI's promotion policy offers a path for high-performing officers to reach senior and top management grades, including opportunities for international postings. SBI PO Exam Preparation 2026: Strategy, Exam Pattern, Selection Process & Study Plan

content_sections.heading: Documents and Identity Proof Required at Examination
content_sections.text: Candidates must carry specific documents at each stage of the selection process, and missing or mismatched documents can lead to disqualification. At the time of the Preliminary and Main Examinations, candidates must bring a valid photo identity proof such as a Passport, Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Driving Licence, Voter's ID Card, or Bank Passbook with an attested photograph, along with a self-attested photocopy. The notification also specifies that candidates must possess a valid Aadhaar card , as SBI conducts online Aadhaar verification at different stages of the selection process, and biometric verification (photo and thumb impression/IRIS scan) may also be carried out. For the Main Exam specifically, candidates must bring the duly authenticated Original Preliminary Exam Call Letter along with the authenticated ID proof copy, the Main Exam Call Letter , and two additional photographs identical to the one used during registration; candidates without these will not be allowed to appear. Use of mobile phones or any electronic devices inside the examination or interview premises is strictly banned and can lead to disqualification.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying for SBI PO 2026
content_sections.text: Many otherwise eligible candidates lose out due to small, avoidable errors during the application process, so it helps to be deliberate at each step. Keep your photograph, signature, thumb impression, and hand-written declaration within the exact pixel dimensions and file-size ranges specified, since uploads outside these limits will trigger an error and block submission; also remember that signatures and the hand-written declaration in capital letters are not accepted. Avoid waiting until the closing date of 8 July 2026 to register, as heavy website traffic close to the deadline is a common cause of failed submissions, and SBI does not take responsibility for applications that fail to go through for this reason. Double-check your category selection before final submission, since no change of category is permitted at any stage after registration, and incorrectly chosen reserved or unreserved status cannot be corrected later. If an online payment fails or appears incomplete, register again and make the payment afresh rather than assuming the transaction went through, since the fee is strictly non-refundable. Finally, save your provisional registration number and password immediately after saving your form, and keep multiple copies of your registration photograph ready, as these are needed at every subsequent stage of the selection process.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for SBI PO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online registration and payment of fee is 8 July 2026; registration opened on 18 June 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in SBI PO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 1500 vacancies for the post of Probationary Officer, comprising 1446 regular and 54 backlog vacancies.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for SBI PO 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 21 and 30 years old as on 01 April 2026, with age relaxation available for OBC, SC/ST, PwBD, and Ex-servicemen categories.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for SBI PO?
faq.answer: Candidates need a Graduation degree in any discipline from a recognised university, with eligibility checked as on 30 September 2026. Final-year students can also apply provisionally.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for SBI PO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is Rs 750 for UR, EWS, and OBC candidates, while SC, ST, and PwBD candidates are exempt from paying any fee.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for SBI PO 2026?
faq.answer: Selection involves three phases: Preliminary Examination, Main Examination, and Psychometric Test/Group Exercise/Interview. Only Main Exam and Interview marks count toward the final merit list.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary of an SBI Probationary Officer?
faq.answer: The starting basic pay is Rs 48,480 (JMGS-I scale), with an approximate CTC of Rs 21.97 Lacs at the Mumbai Centre, inclusive of allowances and benefits.

faq.question: Q8. Is there a bond required after joining as SBI PO?
faq.answer: Yes, selected candidates must execute a bond of Rs 2.00 Lacs to serve the Bank for a minimum of three years from the date of joining.

faq.question: Q9. How can I apply online for SBI PO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/sbipojun26/ or via SBI's official careers page at https://sbi.bank.in/web/careers/current-openings.

faq.question: Q10. Is SBI PO Recruitment conducted by IBPS or by SBI directly?
faq.answer: SBI PO Recruitment 2026 is conducted directly by State Bank of India's Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, though candidate queries and grievances are handled through the IBPS grievance portal at cgrs.ibps.in.
